channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Some US lawmakers want more Christianity in the classroom. Trump could embolden their plans
item.description: Conservative lawmakers across the U.S. are pushing to introduce more Christianity to public school classrooms, testing the separation of church and state.
item.dc:creator: Moriah Balingit, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Winter weather limits North Carolina governor's inauguration to TV speech
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — With an outdoor public inauguration ceremony canceled due to winter weather, new North Carolina Gov. Josh Stein instead planned to kick off his term in office with a televised address indoors.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: LA Times owner: It's 'crazy' we don't elect people with competence
item.description: Los Angeles Times owner Dr. Pat Soon-Shiong said it's "crazy" his state "doesn't elect leaders with competence.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: Wife of American detained in Afghanistan heads to Mar-a-Lago to beg Trump to take up prisoner swap
item.description: A wife desperate to bring her husband home after two-plus years of wrongful detainment in Afghanistan has flown to Mar-a-Lago to implore President-elect Trump to take up her case.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: As Legislature prepares for new session, rifts between parties show a partisan divide
item.description: The Minnesota Legislature will gavel-in a new session on Jan. 14 at noon, and MPR’s politics team is at the Capitol to cover it all. Coming up Monday at 9 a.m., MPR News politics editor Brian Bakst and his guests talk about the 2025 legislative session.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: TikTok gets frosty reception at Supreme Court in fight to stave off ban
item.description: TikTok received a frosty reception in its fight to save the platform at the Supreme Court, which during oral arguments Friday expressed sympathy with the government’s national security concerns about the platform’s ties to China.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, NewsNation

item.title: Tulsi Gabbard changes tune on controversial intelligence tool following GOP lobbying
item.description: Tulsi Gabbard went back on her previous position against section 702 of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act ahead of her confirmation hearing in the U.S. Senate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden extends time in US for 800,000 Venezuelans, Salvadorans as Trump readies immigration crackdown
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — About 600,000 Venezuelans and more than 230,000 Salvadorans already living in the United States can legally remain another 18 months, the Department of Homeland Security said Friday, barely a week before President-elect Donald Trump takes office with promises of hardline immigration pol
item.dc:creator: GISELA SALOMON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump can still vote after sentencing, but can't own a gun and will have to turn over DNA sample
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump doesn't have to go to jail, pay a fine or perform community service as a result of his New York hush money conviction. A judge ended the case Friday with a sentence of an unconditional discharge, closing the case with no punishment.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Investigation clears Duluth mayor’s campaign manager and partner
item.description: An external investigation commissioned by the city of Duluth has found Mayor Roger Reinert’s partner and campaign manager Amber Gurske did not act improperly through her communications with city staff early in his administration.
item.dc:creator: Dan Kraker, MPR News

item.title: Supreme Court appears skeptical of blocking US ban on TiKTok: What to know
item.description: The Supreme Court heard TikTok's case to toss out a ban just nine days before it will take effect. The Biden administration defended the measure on national security grounds.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge holds Rudy Giuliani in contempt of court for continued lies about Georgia election workers
item.description: Rudy Giuliani has been found in contempt of court for continuing to spread lies about two former Georgia election workers after a jury awarded the women a $148 million defamation judgment.
item.dc:creator: Michael Kunzelman, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: President-elect Trump to be sentenced in hush money case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — In a singular moment in U.S. history, President-elect Donald Trump faces sentencing Friday for his New York hush money conviction after the nation's highest court refused to intervene.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: LA Mayor Bass stonewalls reporter for 90 seconds at airport
item.description: Video taken on an airport jetway shows Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass repeatedly ignoring questions from a reporter about her response to the wildfires ripping through the area this week.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, Fox 5

item.title: Trump, Obama exchange pleasantries at Carter funeral after long-standing rivalry
item.description: President-elect Trump and former President Obama had an extended friendly exchange as they sat next to each other at the funeral services for former President Carter on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Democratic senator: Alito has made impropriety the norm
item.description: Sen. Richard Blumenthal (D-Conn.), a member of the Senate Judiciary Committee, on Thursday blasted conservative Justice Samuel Alito for having a conversation with President-elect Trump amidst Trump’s effort to have courts halt his criminal sentencing in New York.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: National Day of Mourning: What's closed as Jimmy Carter is laid to rest
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Some facilities and operations will be closed on Thursday in observance of the National Day of Mourning following former President Jimmy Carter's death. Here's what's closed: All flags will continue to be flown at half-staff for 30 days from Dec.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Ex-FBI informant who fabricated bribery story about Biden and his son Hunter gets 6 years in prison
item.description: A former FBI informant who fabricated a story about President Joe Biden and his son Hunter accepting bribes that became central to Republicans’ impeachment effort was sentenced Wednesday to six years in prison.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New book alleges that someone at Fox News gave Trump's team early look at town hall questions
item.description: Someone inside Fox News gave Donald Trump's presidential campaign the questions he would face ahead of a town hall the network produced in January 2024, a forthcoming book alleges, according to excerpts reported by CNN.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan J. Cooper, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ohio's COVID-era health director becomes first Dem to file for governor
item.description: “I’m a doctor, not a politician,” she said in a statement announcing her candidacy. “I solve problems by bringing people together regardless of party to find solutions.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, NewsNation

item.title: First Jan. 6 felony defendant allowed to attend Trump inauguration
item.description: U.S. District Judge Rudolph Contreras granted William Pope's motion despite objections from the Department of Justice.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Thune tees up Tuberville's bill to block transgender athletes from women’s sports
item.description: Senate Majority Leader John Thune (R-S.D.) on Tuesday took the necessary procedural steps to place Sen. Tommy Tuberville’s (R-Ala.) bill blocking transgender athletes from women’s sports on the Senate calendar. The step sets up a vote on Tuberville’s bill in the weeks ahead.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Durbin asks DOJ to clarify scope of president’s military power ahead of Trump inauguration
item.description: Sen. Dick Durbin (D-Ill.), the top Democrat on the Senate Judiciary Committee, is urging the Department of Justice (DOJ) to rescind a number of internal opinions dealing with a president’s military powers while asking for clarification on domestic use of America’s forces.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Garland to release Smith's Jan. 6 report, hold back Mar-a-Lago report
item.description: Attorney General Merrick Garland plans to release only the volume of special counsel Jack Smith’s report dealing with Donald Trump’s plans to subvert the transfer of power after his loss in the 2020 election, holding back on sharing the Mar-a-Lago report while the president-elect's two co-defendants
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: 5 takeaways from Trump's Mar-a-Lago press conference
item.description: Trump's remarks were typically wide-ranging and encompassed topics from conflicts in the Middle East and Ukraine to Trump’s long-standing grievances.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, NewsNation

item.title: Takeaways from Trump’s Mar-a-Lago press conference
item.description: In a press conference at his Mar-a-Lago club in Florida, Trump would not rule out using military force to seize control of the Panama Canal and Greenland, citing national security interests.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump won't commit to not using military to get Panama Canal
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump on Tuesday refused to commit to not using the U.S. military to gain control of the Panama Canal, after vowing last month to take over the operation of the key passageway.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Board of Supervisors splits votes on new chair following Vargas' exit
item.description: The San Diego County Board of Supervisors punted a vote on who will succeed Nora Vargas as chair after competing motions to elevate either Terra Lawson-Remer or Jim Desmond to the role failed in votes down partisan lines.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: Trump refuses to rule out use of military force to take control of Greenland, Panama Canal
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump on Tuesday said he would not rule out the use of military force to seize control of the Panama Canal and Greenland, as he declared U.S. control of both to be vital to American national security.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and ZEKE MILLER Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Jimmy Carter eulogized by Kamala Harris and others as 39th president returns to Washington
item.description: Nearly 44 years after Jimmy Carter left the nation's capital in humbling defeat, the 39th president returned to Washington for three days of state funeral rites starting Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Hush money judge refuses to delay Trump's criminal sentencing
item.description: The New York judge who oversaw President-elect Trump’s criminal trial last spring declined his request to pause Friday’s sentencing, setting up a last-minute appellate battle as he seeks to stave off the proceeding.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Canada's Trudeau announces resignation after nearly a decade as prime minister
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau announced his resignation Monday after nearly a decade in power, bowing to rising discontent over his leadership and growing turmoil within his government signaled by the abrupt departure of his finance minister.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump pitches 'merged' US, Canada after Trudeau resignation announcement
item.description: After Trudeau’s announcement Monday, Trump touted the idea that many Canadians would “LOVE” the idea of being the "51st State" of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Millions of public workers are set to get higher Social Security benefits. Here's why
item.description: Public service workers, including some teachers, firefighters and police officers, may soon see their Social Security payments increase by hundreds of dollars monthly.
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim, MPR News

item.title: Wisconsin’s Evers revives plan to enable voters to repeal and create state laws
item.description: Wisconsin Gov. Tony Evers is reviving his plan to enable voters to repeal and create state laws, this time through the state budget.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump is certified as the 2024 election winner without challenge, in stark contrast to 2021 violence
item.description: Lawmakers convened under heavy security and a snowstorm to certify the vote, with the reading of the electoral results from the states coming swiftly.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Biden blocks some new offshore drilling ahead of Trump takeover
item.description: President Biden has blocked new offshore drilling for oil and gas in several parts of the country about two weeks before President-elect Trump takes office. Biden has announced that he’ll block new drilling off the entire East Coast, as well as California, Oregon, and Washington state.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, WFLA, NBC
